Comment: Honestly he came off as intimidating at first, but he quickly softened up. He's actually a nice guy and you could tell he's very passionate about the material and knows what he's talking about. Assignments were quick and the labs were easy to follow along. Him and the TA were very kind and helpful. However, beware he does call on people.
Tags: Gives good feedback, Online Savvy

Comment: Excellent professor, but be ready to work. His classes go deep and you learn skills that last. Many who struggle never ask for help, but he's extremely willing if you do. He puts real effort into the course, and if you show interest and work hard, he's one of the best at UM.
Tags: Gives good feedback

Comment: Yeah this guy has the most condescending tone as a professor. Very harsh grader and will not accept any late submissions even if you went to the hospital for a kidney stone, which makes no sense since the TA is the one that grades the HW and Labs. Also likes to nitpick on trivial stuff alot.
Tags: Tough grader

Comment: This professor is not easy. He is not overly difficult either. I took processors with him and it was a difficult concept that he broke down into very understandable chunks. He used the textbook and went off it when necessary. His grading policy is clear and his teaching slides/lectures are helpful. Not one for humor though.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Beware of pop quizzes, Lecture heavy

Comment:
Genuinely among the best professors in the Engineering school.
Authentically cares that his students get something out of the class and holds himself to a standard reflecting as much.
He's also truly a good teacher, which helps.
The workload is reasonable enough, and he's willing to work with the class if they disagree.
Tags: Gives good feedback, Inspirational, Caring

Comment: He always asked me to shut up when I asked questions. But he helped me find myself when I got lost in assembly language.

Was my least favorite prof before asking for help. The subject matter is difficult. After reaching out he has done everything in his power to help me and I could tell genuinely wanted me to succeed, even tho I was rude to him in prior classes. Baseline the class is very HARD but if you need help ASK for it. Show him you care and you will do well!
Tags: Gives good feedback, Skip class? You won't pass., Group projects

Comment: Creates tests that even honor students find difficult, tests always averaging about 70 something for whole class, curves at the end. Unnecessary research paper for course, claiming "writing requirement from the university" when there is no mention of the requirement anywhere on canelink. Will not accept late homework, even if it had a valid reason.
Tags: Lots of homework, So many papers, Tough grader

Comment: When I say "hilarious," I mean hilariously bad. We spent 3 weeks on a 1 week lab all because he sent us bad files, and he insisted he sent good files until he tried to do the lab himself and failed. We had 3 homeworks for the first 13 weeks of class, and then he assigned 3 more for the last 2 weeks of class (probably forgot to assign them earlier).
Tags: Hilarious

Comment: Sets you up for failure, he will deduct points from everything just because he feels like it and when asked why he would just say "thats the grade you got" with out even explaining why you're wrong. BEWARE. you MUST learn everything on your own and waste precious study time sitting in has class watching him struggle to solve his own problems
Tags: Test heavy, Beware of pop quizzes, Tough grader

Comment: TA is as confused as we are about how to complete the labs. It's 'easy! took me 15 minutes!', comes here after we fail to figure anything out in 3 lab sessions and fails to achieve anything himself. Suddenly it takes him 5 days to do it. Routinely late to class, uploading assignments, and sending labs to TA, yet will screw you if you are late
Tags: Beware of pop quizzes, Tough grader

Comment: Reads off the power points provided by book publisher, says 'it's not within the scope of this course' whenever you ask ANY questions. Gets lost in explanations of problems, says figure it out. When he posts homework answers, everyone is confused as to how we were expected to understand what he wanted from the questions. For tests memorize the ppts
Tags: Beware of pop quizzes, Tough grader

Comment: Assembly was taught in this class, but it was not taught well. Many of my classmates and I agree that we didn't learn anything. The professor struggled to explain concepts further when students had questions that he couldn't simply answer by reading from the slides. Answer key was wrong for a midterm question. Passed, but felt like I didn't learn.
Doesn't explain things well. I didn't learn much in lectures, and had to learn everything through reading the textbook.

One of the best professors Ive had, really cares about the learning experience and provides helpful videos that show you how to do the problems. He is very reasonable and the class is hard but he will prepare you for the tests. He also offers extra credit opportunities.
Tags: Accessible outside class, Clear grading criteria, Caring

Comment: If you want to learn assembly code, take his class.
Tags: Inspirational, EXTRA CREDIT

Comment: he only has pop quizzes, they follow his presentations, but you need to review all the slides. he is very accessible and flexible.
Tags: Participation matters, BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES, ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

Comment: A lot of homework and quizzes (lowest 2 dropped). He asks you to read sections of the chapters before the lecture. In class, the quizzes are simple and based on what you read, and they count for attendance. You will solve problems in groups and he walks around answering questions. If you like that style of teaching, you will do really well.
Tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK, EXTRA CREDIT, GROUP PROJECTS

Comment: If nothing else, you will learn how to properly format an IEEE paper in this course!
Tags: Gives good feedback, Amazing lectures

Comment: The labs are easy and if you do them you will nail the programming questions on the exams. He gives quizzes as extra credit to the exams. If you write a good paper he will encourage you to pursue an experiment and some really good feedback.
Tags: Gives good feedback, Inspirational, EXTRA CREDIT

Comment: He relies heavily on the textbook. If you read the textbook with a microscope and do all of the example exercises back and forth (and 20 times over in your sleep), you'll get an A. His finals are extremely difficult and completely comprehensive. Make sure you read even the corners of the textbook that are in 10pt font.
Tags: Tough Grader, Get ready to read, TEST HEAVY

Comment: The class was fun.
Tags: Caring, EXTRA CREDIT, LECTURE HEAVY

Comment: Cares if we understood him
Tags: Gives good feedback, Respected, EXTRA CREDIT

Comment: For homework, he pulls questions from the textbook but makes us submit our answers through blackboard, which gives very little feedback besides which answers were right or wrong. For the final, he basically took the hardest and most missed questions from each exam, made them harder, and included new material he  didn't really cover in class.
Tags: Tough Grader, Skip class? You won't pass., BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

Comment: One of the worst classes I've taken. He tells students to ask their questions later but then doesn't have time & when we do get to ask them he doesn't understand what we're asking. Very difficult grader-if you get something wrong in pt a) he'll take points off in each subsequent section. Told us we didn't need ch 10 for final but it was 30% ch 10.
Tags: Tough Grader, Skip class? You won't pass., BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES
